landing.title: What is civilization made of?
landing.subtitle: Explore the complete tree of human technologies, from raw materials to finished products.
landing.searchPlaceholder: Type an invention: Steel, Smartphone, Bread...
landing.howStep1Desc: Type any technology in the search bar.
landing.howStep2Desc: View the materials, tools, and energy needed to make it.
landing.howStep3Desc: Navigate layer by layer down to raw materials.
landing.featureHighlightBody: {name} requires more than {rawCount} raw materials and {layers} layers of transformation.
landing.howDemoHint: Click an invention to explore the full tree.
landing.quoteSagan: If you wish to make an apple pie from scratch, you must first invent the universe.

explore.cardNoImageAria: No image for this invention yet.
explore.linkNeighborhoodSelectAria: How to show related cards in the tree and detail
explore.layerBadgeTitle: Layer {layer}: depth in the tree (steps from raw materials).
explore.badgeBuiltUponHelpBody: It is the number of direct upstream inputs for this invention — each card or material linked in “Built upon” counts as one. Current count: {count}.
explore.suggestDeleteLinkTitle: Suggest removing the link between {source} and {target}?
explore.treeNavMainCardTitle: Scroll to the main invention card
explore.builtUponTotalCards: {count, plural, one {# card required upstream} other {# cards required upstream}}
explore.builtUponHowIntro: Each invention is sorted into six groups (plus the central card):
explore.builtUponHowMatter: Matter — what things are made of. Under Matters, three columns: raw → processed → industrial. Component-level matter and the “component” dimension appear in the Components box.
explore.builtUponHowProcess: Process — how things are transformed (techniques, procedures).
explore.builtUponHowTool: Tools & machines — what we transform with (equipment, machines).
explore.builtUponHowLevels: Three levels under Matters; Components, Energy, and Infrastructure each have their own box.
explore.ledToTotalCards: {count, plural, one {# card downstream (transitive)} other {# cards downstream (transitive)}}
explore.suggestTagsHint: Type to search existing tags, click one to add, or press Enter to add a new tag. Click a tag chip to remove it.
explore.suggestEraHint: Era used for classification (not shown on the explorer detail card).
explore.legendMattersSubtitle: What is the object made of?
explore.legendProcessExamples: Metal melting, Silicon refining, Assembly, Plastic injection molding
explore.legendToolsSubtitle: What tools and machines do we use to transform it?
explore.legendToolsExamples: Blast furnace, CNC machine, Assembly robot, Factory
explore.legendOriginsSubtitle: Where does it come from in nature?
explore.legendNatureRow1c3: Consists of a single type of atom
explore.legendNatureRow2c2: Two or more elements chemically bonded
explore.legendNatureRow3c2: Substance or mixture used in fabrication

categoriesPage.metaDescription: Choose a category or an era to browse related technologies and open them in the tree.
categoriesPage.title: Categories
categoriesPage.tabPickerAriaLabel: Switch between category view and era view
categoriesPage.intro: Pick a category (e.g. Food) to see every card in that category, then open an invention in the tree.
categoriesPage.introEra: Pick an era (e.g. Industrial) to see every matching technology, then open an invention in the tree.
categoriesPage.listSubtitleMaterialLevel: All technologies for this material level.
categoriesPage.listSubtitleInventionKind: All technologies of this invention type.
categoriesPage.eraSortAriaLabel: Sort by category or by decade
categoriesPage.eraCategoryAnchorsAria: Jump to a category section on this page
categoriesPage.eraDecadeAnchorsAria: Jump to a decade section on this page
categoriesPage.categoryListNoSearchMatch: No technologies match your search in this list.

editor.title: Editor
editor.name: Name
editor.confirmDelete: Delete this invention? This will also remove associated links.
editor.adminOnly: This page is restricted to administrators.
editor.rowActionCompareDuplicates: Compare the two duplicate cards side by side
editor.isolatedOnlyTitle: Show only inventions with no incoming or outgoing links
editor.duplicatesOnlyTitle: Show only cards with a duplicate name or English name
editor.detailDuplicateOpenAdminTitle: Open in editor with duplicate filter and scroll to the other card
editor.detailDuplicateNoPeer: No duplicate card (same name or English name as another entry)
editor.filterNoImageTitle: Show only inventions without an image
editor.filterNoDescriptionTitle: Show only inventions without an English description
editor.filterIncompleteTitle: Show only inventions missing at least one of: English description, dimension, material level, category, or year
editor.filterDeadEndsTitle: Show only inventions with at least one incoming link but no outgoing links (dead ends)
editor.sortByDepthTitle: Sort by number of direct upstream cards (same as the explore badge) — lowest first when ascending
editor.deleteConfirmRich: Delete {name}? This will also remove {count, plural, one {# associated link} other {# associated links}}. This cannot be undone.
editor.toastFixFormFields: Please fix the fields marked in red.
editor.fieldErrorYearInvalid: Invalid year: use an integer or leave empty.
editor.addCardHintName: Official or common name of the invention or concept as shown on the card.
editor.addCardHintDate: Approximate year (e.g. -3000 for 3000 BCE). Leave empty if unknown.
editor.addCardHintCategory: Main domain: drives the card color and how it is grouped in the tree.
editor.addCardHintNature: For materials and compounds: natural origin and chemical nature when relevant.
editor.addCardHintTags: Short keywords (comma-separated) for filters and search.
editor.addCardHintEra: Historical era; use together with the year to place the invention in time.
editor.addCardHintOrigin: Place, people, civilization, or context of emergence if known.
editor.addCardHintDescription: A few sentences on what this invention is and its role in a manufacturing chain.
editor.addCardHintLinks: Connect the card to the graph: downstream outputs and upstream dependencies.
editor.unresolvedSuggestedLinkBadge: unresolved in the graph — search for a card to create the link
editor.unresolvedCardCreateOnApproveHint: If the card does not exist in the database yet, approval can create it, enrich it, and link it to this card.
editor.toastNameAlreadyExists: A card with this name already exists
editor.toastWikimediaNoImage: No Wikipedia image was found for this card.
editor.selectedCount: {count, plural, one {# card selected} other {# cards selected}}
editor.toastCompareLocked: Cannot compare: one of the cards is locked.
editor.adminPanelLockedNotice: This card is locked: editing is disabled. Unlock it from the table (lock icon).
editor.suggestAddLinkAria: Add this invention to the suggestion
editor.aiReviewToastCreated: {count, plural, one {# suggestion created} other {# suggestions created}}
editor.aiReviewBatchScopeHint: Narrow the batch by category, era, and/or year range (year_approx). Leave blank to not restrict.
editor.aiReviewBatchMaxCardsHint: Leave empty for no limit (all cards matching filters).
editor.aiReviewBatchMaxCardsHintExplicit: Applied after resolving names/ids (optional cap on the explicit list).
editor.aiReviewBatchIdOrNameList: Cards to analyze (ids or exact names)
editor.aiReviewBatchIdOrNameListPlaceholder: One id or full card name per line, or comma-separated
editor.aiReviewBatchIdOrNameListHint: Names must match a single card (exact id, exact title, or unambiguous partial match).
editor.aiReviewBatchExplicitCallout: Batch preset, category, era, years, and graph depth filters are ignored — only the list below is used (plus publication / locked rules and max cards).
editor.aiReviewBatchComplexityDisabledHint: Not applied when using an explicit list.
editor.aiReviewBatchNoIdsEstimate: Estimate the batch first (button above), or paste one or more ids or exact card names.
editor.aiReviewBatchNoIdsExplicitEmpty: No valid entries in the list — add at least one id or card name.
editor.aiReviewBatchMissingTitle: Names not found in the database
editor.aiReviewBatchMissingTitleAmbiguous: List entries without a single matching card
editor.aiReviewBatchUnresolvedIntro: These entries do not match any card (they are skipped in analysis until they exist):
editor.aiReviewBatchAmbiguousIntro: Ambiguous — several cards match; narrow the name or pick a card in the tree:
editor.aiReviewBatchAmbiguousRenamePlaceholder: Exact title or id to use instead…
editor.aiReviewBatchSeedMissingButton: Create missing cards as drafts, auto-fill with AI, save
editor.aiReviewBatchSeedMissingToast: {n} draft card(s) created. Estimate again to refresh the list.
editor.aiReviewBatchSeedMissingHintAmbiguous: For each label below, you can add a new draft with that exact title (in addition to existing similar cards), then estimate again.
editor.aiToolsIntro: Generate drafts, enqueue enrichment suggestions, fix images from Wikipedia, and run batch AI review from one place.
editor.aiToolsCardAddDesc: Draft nodes and material links (Claude Haiku), with optional cascade for missing ingredients.
editor.aiToolsCardEnrichDesc: Enrichment suggestions (same approval flow as edits) based on missing fields.
editor.aiToolsCardImagesDesc: Preview image URLs then apply them to cards without an image.
editor.aiToolsCardReviewDesc: Estimate a filtered batch, or paste ids or exact card names, then run analysis.
editor.aiToolsPanelReviewIntro: Walks the batch from filters or from an explicit list (ids or exact card names) and creates “AI Review” suggestions: fields, links, graph consistency. For filter-based batches, estimate cost first to lock the list.
editor.aiToolsPanelAddIntro: Generates draft cards and material links for a category and time span. Links to existing cards can skip locked or draft nodes.
editor.aiToolsPanelEnrichIntro: Proposes enrichments for existing cards (fields and links), processing the most incomplete cards first. Suggestions follow the same approval flow as edits.
editor.aiToolsPanelImagesIntro: Resolves images from Wikipedia (article URL, or name search when allowed), then updates images after preview and selection.
editor.aiToolsPanelBulletIdList: Id list: {count} id(s) pasted (still filtered like the rest).
editor.aiToolsPanelBulletExplicitListDetail: Explicit list: {count} card(s) by id or exact name (batch filters are ignored).
editor.aiToolsPanelBulletExplicitListEmpty: Explicit list: no valid entries yet — add ids or names.
editor.aiToolsPanelBulletMaxCardsExplicitList: At most {n} cards from the resolved explicit list.
editor.aiToolsPanelBulletNoEstimateBypass: You can run without estimating when the list above is filled (estimate is optional for explicit lists).
editor.aiToolsPanelBulletLockedExcl: Locked cards: excluded from the batch.
editor.aiToolsPanelBulletLockedIncl: Locked cards: not excluded by this filter.
editor.aiToolsPanelBulletNoEstimate: Run “Estimate cost” to show the exact card count and price.
editor.aiToolsPanelAddCascadeOn: Cascade: drafts may be created for missing ingredients.
editor.aiToolsPanelAddBulletLinkLocked: Links: do not target locked cards.
editor.aiToolsPanelAddBulletLinkLockedOff: Links: locked cards may be targeted.
editor.aiToolsPanelEnrichBulletPool: Last count: {n} matching card(s) in the pool.
editor.aiToolsPanelImagesBulletLimit: Preview: up to {n} card(s) per batch.
editor.aiToolsPanelImagesBulletWikiOnly: Resolution: Wikipedia URL only (no name search).
editor.aiToolsPanelImagesBulletWikiFallback: Resolution: Wikipedia URL when present, else name search.
editor.aiToolsPanelImagesBulletAnyImage: Cards with or without an image (per filter).
editor.aiToolsPanelImagesBulletDry: Last estimate: {resolved} preview row(s) out of {pool} eligible, ~ €{eur}.
editor.aiToolsAddExcludeLocked: Do not link to a locked card
editor.aiToolsAddExcludeDraftLinks: Do not link to a draft card
editor.aiToolsEnrichPriorityHint: Most incomplete cards are processed first (missing-field score).
editor.aiToolsReviewIdListPlaceholder: id1, id2 or one id per line
editor.aiToolsEnrichHint: Pick missing-field criteria, then run (or dry-run). Suggestions appear under Suggestions.
editor.aiToolsFilterNoDimension: Missing dimension, or matter card missing level / natural origin / chemical nature
editor.aiToolsImagesHint: Preview is read-only: resolve via the card’s Wikipedia article, else search by name. Select rows, then apply.

about.title: About Craftree
about.subtitle: Understanding what civilization is made of.
about.projectP1: Craftree is an interactive technology tree that models human inventions as fabrication recipes.
about.projectP2: Unlike classic timelines that show when things were invented, Craftree answers a different question: what do you need to make them?
about.projectP3: Each invention is broken down into three dimensions: the materials it's made of, the processes used to transform them, and the tools and machines required. Click on any input to explore its own recipe, all the way down to raw materials found in nature — sand, clay, wood, water, fire.
about.projectP4: The result is an inverted pyramid. At the top, a smartphone. At the bottom, sand and ore. In between, hundreds of inventions stacked on one another, each made possible by those before it. Craftree makes that depth visible and explorable.
about.projectP5: The project was born from a conviction: in a world where everything moves faster, it matters to understand where things come from — not only who invented them, but what had to be assembled, transformed, and mastered for them to exist.
about.graphP1: Each invention sits at the center of the page. Above it, you see everything it enables — the inventions it contributes to. Below it, you see everything it requires — its full fabrication recipe.
about.graphP2: The page is organized around three horizontal rows:
about.dimensionMattersBody: What is the object made of? These are the physical substances and components: sand, copper, steel, batteries, processors. Within this row, materials are sorted into four levels — raw materials, processed materials, industrial materials, and components — reflecting how far they've been transformed from their natural state.
about.dimensionProcessBody: How do we transform it? These are the techniques and methods: smelting, refining, forging, assembly, chemical synthesis.
about.dimensionToolsBody: What do we use to transform it? These are the instruments of production: furnaces, CNC machines, assembly robots, factories.
about.materialLevelsIntro: Materials follow a progression from nature to function:
about.materialRawP: Raw materials — Extracted directly from nature with no transformation. Iron ore, sand, crude oil, wood.
about.materialProcessedP: Processed materials — A new substance created through a fundamental transformation. Steel, silicon, plastic, flour. You measure them by weight or volume.
about.materialIndustrialP: Industrial materials — A known material shaped or calibrated for a specific use. Copper wire, steel sheet, tempered glass. Same substance, but prepared.
about.materialComponentsP: Components — A functional piece that does something on its own. A battery stores energy. A processor computes. An engine converts fuel into motion. You count them as units, not by weight.
about.collaborativeP1: Craftree is built and enriched by its community. Anyone can suggest corrections, propose new inventions, or add missing links between technologies. Every suggestion is reviewed before being published.
about.collaborativeP2: The data is never finished — it grows with each contribution, like an encyclopedia of making.
about.behindP1: Craftree is an independent project created by Julien Beljio. It is not affiliated with any company, university, or institution. The code is open source and the data is freely accessible.
about.behindP2: If you'd like to support the project, you can contribute content, share it with others, or buy me a coffee.
